title: Aspilia tenella
taxonomy: Aspilia tenella is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a part of the genus Aspilia, which comprises various species of plants native to tropical regions. The plant is a member of the tribe Heliantheae, which is a group of plants with daisy-like flowers. Aspilia tenella is further classified as belonging to the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants with composite flowers. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family.
uses: Aspilia tenella, also known as African wormweed, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In some parts of the world, the leaves and stems of the plant are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Additionally, Aspilia tenella is sometimes used to treat digestive problems and to support the immune system. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
